html: Cuttings in the flesh, or the laceration of one�s body for the "propitiation of their gods," (1�Kings 18:28) constituted a prominent feature of idolatrous worship, especially among the Syrians. The Israelites were prohibited from indulging in such practices. (Leviticus 19:28; 21:5; 14:1; Jeremiah 16:6)
